summarization.a: The subfigure shows a side view of the DCS–amine adduct, highlighting the bonding between the amine nitrogen and the silicon center, along with surrounding Cl and H atoms. The spatial arrangement makes the coordination geometry and nearby Wannier centers visible.
summarization.b: The subfigure presents a top view of the same DCS–amine adduct on the surface, showing how the amine group sits relative to the surrounding lattice and neighboring atoms.

a.question: How does the side view in subfigure (a) illustrate the interaction between the amine group and the silicon center?
a.answer: The side view shows the amine nitrogen directly coordinated to the silicon atom, forming the DCS–amine adduct. The surrounding Cl and H atoms define the local bonding environment, making it clear how the amine approaches and binds to the silicon site.

a.question: Which atom in subfigure (a) directly bonds with the amine nitrogen?

b.question: What viewing orientation is shown in subfigure (b)?
Does subfigure (b) show how the DCS–amine adduct is positioned relative to the surrounding surface atoms?
